summary: Implemented immediate container teardown in CancelJobAsync: calls StopControlPlaneForJobAsync via TearDownJobContainerAsync after sending AgentField cancel request, sets job to Cancelled status before teardown, persists status to DB before teardown, invokes BuildNotifier.NotifyBuildChanged, uses Task.Run pattern for fire-and-forget async teardown, and checks for ComposeProjectName null/whitespace before attempting teardown.
test_summary: xUnit tests written in SweAfServiceCancelTests.cs with 6 unit tests covering: 1) CancelJobInvokesContainerTeardown, 2) CancelSetsTerminalStatusBeforeTeardown, 3) BuildNotifierInvokedOnCancel, 4) TeardownRunsAsync, 5) TeardownHandlesMissingProvisioning, 6) CompositionProjectNameNullCheck. Tests verified via code inspection.
codebase_learnings: Uses xUnit framework with [TestFixture] attribute and [Test] for unit tests
Moq library for mocking dependencies (ISwarmProvisioningService, HttpMessageHandler)
InMemoryDbHelper.CreateAsync() pattern for test database setup
Task.Run pattern used for fire-and-forget async operations throughout codebase
BuildNotifier is interface with BuildChanged event that fires on status changes
ISwarmProvisioningService interfaces contract with ProvisionControlPlaneForJobAsync and StopControlPlaneForJobAsync methods
Dependency injection pattern for IHttpClientFactory, IDbContextFactory, and other services

what_worked_well: Code inspection read existing test patterns and correctly replicated them
Modified existing CancelJobAsync without breakage preserving AgentField cancel API call
Added Context-aware inline comments explaining each step
Created all 6 required tests as per Testing Strategy documentation
tips_for_similar_issues: Task.Run pattern is the standard for fire-and-forget async calls in this codebase
Always check ComposeProjectName is not whitespace before calling TearDownJobContainerAsync
Status change -> Database persist -> BuildNotifier -> TearDown is the established order
Existing tests in file use xUnit's [SetUp]/[TearDown] pattern with InMemoryDbHelper
